title: File I/O & Data Handlers
description: Generic file I/O abstraction layer for reading/writing structured data files (CSV, TXT). Both Lyra-1 and Lyra-2 share identical implementations.
actors: ExternalCaller (application / pipeline code using the handlers)

use_cases.name: Load data from file object
use_cases.notes: TxtHandler returns raw string (file.read()); CsvHandler returns list-of-lists via csv.reader

use_cases.name: Dump data to file object
use_cases.notes: TxtHandler converts obj to str then file.write(); CsvHandler validates list-of-lists then csv.writer.writerows()

use_cases.name: Dump data to string
use_cases.notes: TxtHandler: str(obj); CsvHandler: writes via StringIO, returns getvalue()

use_cases.name: Load data from file path
use_cases.notes: Opens file then delegates to load_from_fileobj(). Concrete behavior is handler-specific.

use_cases.name: Dump data to file path
use_cases.notes: Opens file then delegates to dump_to_fileobj(). Concrete behavior is handler-specific.

relationships: TxtHandler extends BaseFileHandler (inherits UC4, UC5; overrides UC1, UC2, UC3)
CsvHandler extends BaseFileHandler (inherits UC4, UC5; overrides UC1, UC2, UC3)

key_classes.name: CsvHandler
key_classes.write_input: list[list] — raises ValueError if rows are not lists

important_notes: Both Lyra-1 and Lyra-2 have identical TxtHandler and CsvHandler implementations (only import path differs). BaseFileHandler.str_like=True means StringIO (not BytesIO) is used when buffering. CsvHandler enforces row validation (list check) on write but not on read.
